aperture: all robot form factors, full stack
chokepoints: TSMC — leading-edge fab + CoWoS advanced packaging; CoWoS 'sold out through 2026' makes it the single hardest bottleneck gating every AI/robot compute chip
SK Hynix — HBM memory (58% share); every Nvidia GPU generation depends on it for the bandwidth robot brains need
Nvidia — Jetson Thor is the default robot-brain socket across nearly every humanoid launch, plus GR00T brain and DRIVE AV compute; also funds its own demand via NVentures
Arm Holdings — CPU IP embedded in most robot SoCs; a licensing chokepoint even competitors depend on
Harmonic Drive Systems — invented and dominates strain-wave reducers (~14 per humanoid); high-end joint precision is near-impossible to second-source quickly
Nabtesco — RV cycloidal reducer leader (35-60% share) feeding FANUC/Yaskawa/ABB/KUKA; co-locks the high end with Harmonic Drive
GSA + Rollvis — Swiss planetary roller screws, 50%+ of world market; gate humanoid linear actuators (Tesla buys 14 GSA screws per Optimus)
maxon group — 39 precision motors per da Vinci; a component vendor that effectively gates surgical-robot market leader Intuitive Surgical
Physical Intelligence / Skild AI / Google DeepMind Gemini Robotics — the small set of body-agnostic 'default brain' model vendors integrators license rather than build
Hesai + RoboSense — Chinese lidar duopoly supplying the 'eyes' for most humanoid, quadruped and AV programs worldwide
Auterion — Skynode autonomy/strike-kit software standardizing FPV drone brains at mass scale
Intuitive Surgical — 10,670+ da Vinci installed base creates a switching-cost moat over hospital soft-tissue surgery
KION Group / AutoStore — control the grid-ASRS platform and integration channel underlying grocery/retail fulfillment
DJI — ~70-80% global commercial/consumer drone share with deep vertical integration
Amazon Robotics — 1M+ captive fulfillment robots; closed vertical that sets the pace for logistics automation
commodityLayers: FPV / attritable strike-drone airframes (Neros Archer, AV Skyfall Shrike) — deliberately low-cost and mass-produced (~1M units/yr targets); the value migrates to the autonomy kit (Auterion), not the airframe
Sidewalk last-mile delivery robots (Serve, Starship, Coco) — hardware is largely interchangeable; differentiation is fleet ops and platform partnerships, not the chassis
Mid-tier / Chinese cobot arms (Dobot, AUBO, JAKA, Rokae) — three Chinese makers now in the global cobot top 4 on price; the cobot arm itself is commoditizing beneath Universal Robots
Chinese precision reducers & roller screws (Leaderdrive, Wuzhou Xinchun, Zhaowei) — undercutting Japanese/Swiss incumbents ~50% on price; commoditizing the mid/low end of the actuation stack
Chinese GPU 'Nvidia substitutes' (Moore Threads, Biren, Cambricon) — competing primarily on price/sovereignty as fungible domestic alternatives, though gated by SMIC capacity

Volume-tier robot batteries (CATL) and battery thermal/safety packs (KULR, explicitly tagged 'commodity') — scale-driven, low-margin cells; premium/solid-state (Samsung SDI, LG Energy Solution) is where any pricing power sits
Standard grippers / end-of-arm tooling (OnRobot, Zimmer, and much of SCHUNK's catalog) — broad interchangeable modular EOAT; only specialized force/tactile sensing retains a moat
Automotive lidar mid-field (Luminar, tagged hype-risk) — commoditizing as Chinese suppliers (Hesai, RoboSense) scale volume and drive down unit price
Fleet/observability middleware (InOrbit, Formant, Formic) — hardware-agnostic orchestration software with low switching-cost differentiation versus platform incumbents (Intrinsic, Vention)

earlyInsightCandidates.whyEarly: The 'Android of military drones' — Skynode autonomy now going onto 50,000 Ukraine FPV drones, with US DoD adoption and rare non-dilutive US Office of Strategic Capital money. Smart defense money treats it as the software chokepoint for attritable mass, yet the public knows the drone makers, not the brain inside them.
earlyInsightCandidates.watchFor: A NATO/US standardization or block-buy naming Skynode as the reference autonomy kit, or a second nation-scale FPV contract — that converts it from vendor to standard.

earlyInsightCandidates.whyEarly: SoftBank-led with Nvidia, Bezos, Samsung, LG, Schneider and Salesforce all in — the strategics are betting it becomes the one model that runs any robot body, and it already went $0→~$30M revenue. Public awareness is near zero versus Figure/Tesla.
earlyInsightCandidates.watchFor: Named blue-chip OEM design wins (a major integrator shipping on 'Skild Brain') or a revenue figure crossing ~$100M — proof the omni-body thesis is being bought, not just funded.

earlyInsightCandidates.whyEarly: Alphabet's CapitalG led it and the valuation reportedly doubled to ~$11B in four months, yet it builds no robot body — it's the invisible layer researchers rate highest. It open-sourced pi0 to seed ecosystem lock-in, a classic pre-standard move.
earlyInsightCandidates.watchFor: A marquee hardware maker licensing pi models as its production brain, or a paid enterprise deployment — the signal it's becoming the layer others depend on.

earlyInsightCandidates.whyEarly: These two Swiss firms hold 50%+ of planetary roller screws and Tesla sources 14 per Optimus — a hard physical chokepoint on every humanoid's linear actuators. Almost no public or investor awareness; the ultimate picks-and-shovels.
earlyInsightCandidates.watchFor: Optimus (and other humanoid) mass-production ramp confirmations, or capacity-expansion/long-term-supply announcements — demand visibility is the trigger, before Chinese undercutters (Wuzhou Xinchun, Xinjian) compress the window.

earlyInsightCandidates.whyEarly: NVentures, Amazon Industrial Innovation Fund, Samsung Next and Salesforce backed a manipulation model hitting 99%+ task success over 24h continuous runs — the reliability bar that actually unlocks commercial deployment. Very low public profile versus flashier humanoid brains.
earlyInsightCandidates.watchFor: First multi-site commercial contract or a named logistics customer at scale — reliability translating into recurring revenue is the buy trigger.

earlyInsightCandidates.whyEarly: Profitable-trajectory controller software ('make any generic arm autonomous') with sales doubling and doubling again, raising toward a $233M Series D and an IPO by 2030. A boring, high-leverage layer the market ignores while chasing humanoids.
earlyInsightCandidates.watchFor: An IPO filing or a US/European multi-warehouse rollout — the moment its controller becomes the default abstraction layer for mixed-brand arms.

earlyInsightCandidates.whyEarly: Raised Germany's largest-ever round (~$1.4B) with Tether, Amazon, Nvidia and Qualcomm, plus a Bosch co-development deal — Europe's best-funded humanoid, and its Neuraverse aims at fleet-wide network effects. Under-followed in US media relative to its backing.
earlyInsightCandidates.watchFor: Bosch moving from co-development to production orders, or evidence Neuraverse is compounding across a real deployed fleet — network effects are the durability trigger.

earlyInsightCandidates.whyEarly: $15B, BlackRock/Kleiner-backed, embedded in 18 of the top 20 automakers and quietly expanding into defense/off-road — the validation-and-simulation toll road under many autonomy programs. Enterprise-invisible to the public.
earlyInsightCandidates.watchFor: A large defense/off-road autonomy award or an IPO — either surfaces it from infrastructure to headline name.

earlyInsightCandidates.whyEarly: Samsung-backed Rebellions ($400M, KOSPI IPO plan), Furiosa and DeepX (~$700M IPO planned) are the edge-inference layer for physical AI, riding Korea's $649B/$1T national push — a whole national bet the US public isn't watching.
earlyInsightCandidates.watchFor: IPO pricing plus a design win in a shipping robot/vehicle (not just data-center inference) — that confirms the robotics-edge thesis, not just AI-chip hype.

earlyInsightCandidates.company: Optimus China supply chain (Sanhua, Zhaowei, Xinjian, Mirle)
earlyInsightCandidates.whyEarly: Tesla's $685M Sanhua order and the naming of Zhaowei (dexterous-hand modules) and Xinjian (roller screws, 1M-unit line) reveal who actually gets paid when humanoids scale — visible in Asian listings but off most Western radars.
earlyInsightCandidates.watchFor: Confirmed Optimus production volumes or follow-on component orders — order flow is the leading indicator that scales these suppliers before the robot maker's own economics prove out.

longevityVerdicts.why: Compute + model + simulation flywheel (Jetson Thor, GR00T, Isaac) is the default across form factors, and NVentures buys strategic optionality into the layer above. Hardest position in the pool to dislodge.
Every leading robot brain is fabbed by TSMC and fed by HBM. CoWoS sold out through 2026 makes them the sector's true toll booth regardless of which robot company wins.
Only autonomy player with real, scaling paid-ride volume (~500k/week) and 200M+ miles. Alphabet balance sheet removes funding risk. Robotaxi's clearest survivor.
Real BMW commercial contract and onboard Helix VLA are genuine, but a $39B mark on early deployments prices near-perfection. Winner-candidate with the widest downside if scaling slips.
SoftBank/Nvidia/Bezos/Samsung backing and $0→$30M revenue are strong signals for the omni-body-model thesis, but $14B on 7 months of froth needs named OEM design wins to hold.
Best-regarded generalist VLA (pi models), open-sourced to seed ecosystem lock-in. Chokepoint potential, but $11B assumes it becomes the standard others license — unproven commercially.
Actually ships (world #1 humanoid units, low-cost G1) with a real IPO. Commoditization risk at the low end is the flip side of its cost leadership, but it owns the volume narrative.
$20B Army enterprise deal around Lattice makes it the software backbone of US autonomous defense — a procurement moat competitors can't out-VC.
Hivemind autonomy + V-BAT with a $12.7B valuation, Blackstone financing, and multi-government contracts. Defense budgets, not consumer cycles, underwrite longevity.
Hyundai's balance sheet + Gemini Robotics brain + committed 2026 Atlas production. Best-capitalized Western humanoid path even if unit economics lag Chinese rivals.
Reducer and roller-screw duopolies are the picks-and-shovels that every humanoid needs (14 reducers, 14 roller screws per robot). Chinese undercutting erodes share/margin but not near-term necessity.
10,670+ installed base and switching costs make surgical robotics' leader near-unassailable near-term, though Hugo/Versius Plus/Distalmotion finally open credible challenges.
Absorbing Walmart's robotics business plus $5B+ backlog locks in the largest retail-logistics deployment channel in the pool.
~70-80% global drone share and vertical integration are dominant, but US/allied restrictions structurally cap the addressable market and seed NDAA-compliant rivals (Skydio, Parrot, Anduril).
Enormous attention and a China-heavy Optimus supply chain, but ~20 robotaxis vs 'widespread' claims and unproven Optimus mass production make the outcome binary.
10,000 pre-orders are real demand, but teleoperated 'Expert Mode' backfilling 30-40% of tasks means the autonomous-home-robot promise is unproven at ship.

longevityVerdicts.why: 'Nvidia substitute' valuations and revenue growth are real, but SMIC node constraints cap how much of the narrative they can physically deliver.

longevityVerdicts.company: Genesis AI / TESOLLO / Neros / demo-stage names
longevityVerdicts.why: Impressive demos or single big orders without durable deployment or moat. Attention is borrowed from the category, not earned by traction.
The 'make any arm autonomous' software layer with real economics (Mujin doubling sales; Intrinsic inside Google). Low buzz, high structural leverage.

flags.claim: Licenses the Covariant Brain / RFM-1 robotics foundation model to integrators and logistics operators for warehouse picking, sortation, and depalletizing (presented as an ongoing active business)
flags.issue: Stale/misleading as a present-tense description. In Aug 2024 Amazon executed a 'reverse acquihire': all three co-founders (Abbeel, Chen, Duan) and ~25% of staff moved to Amazon, which received a non-exclusive license to Covariant's models for ~$380M+$20M. A 2025 whistleblower complaint to FTC/SEC/DOJ alleges Covariant is now a 'zombie company' with no public updates since the deal. The 'active licensor to many integrators' framing should be flagged as outdated/unverified for 2026.

Mischaracterizes the Amazon relationship as investor 'backing' when it was actually a talent-and-IP reverse-acquihire (co-founders + staff hired by Amazon, non-exclusive license paid to Covariant), not an equity investment. Should be described as a licensing/acquihire deal, not backing.
